title: NABench Nucleotide Fitness Prediction Assays

data_urls.access_notes: Follow upstream repository instructions for assay files.

instructions: Clone or inspect the NABench repository.
Select assays with sufficient support for train/test split construction.
Preserve reference sequence, mutation position, split protocol, and assay metadata.

recommended_axes.name: mutation_position_distance
recommended_axes.definition: Distance from held-out mutation positions to trained mutation positions.

recommended_axes.name: local_sequence_context
recommended_axes.definition: Similarity of windows around mutated positions.

recommended_axes.name: fitness_extremeness
recommended_axes.definition: Distance from training fitness support; diagnostic/post-hoc unless labels are restricted to training support.

useful_for: Turning random versus contiguous splits into a full mutational spectrum.
Testing nucleotide model extrapolation to unobserved mutational regions.
Negative SPECTRA cases where performance failure is not cleanly novelty-specific.
leakage_risks: Whole-sequence identity can collapse for single-mutant assays.
Fitness-derived axes are post-hoc if held-out labels are used.
Constant predictions can dominate curve interpretation.
